findings.description: No rules were extracted at all for this blob despite an entire approved-gradient-set table (6 distinct gradients with stop values and usage) plus two governing list items. This is a complete clustering miss; at minimum one rule per gradient (or one rule for the approved set + one for masthead-specific usage + one for directionality) should exist.

payload.rule_text: Approved gradient set: Brand Blue→Dark Navy (#00529b→#011E45), Sky Blue→Deep Blue (#358CCB→#003D74), Sunshine→75% Gold (#faa31b→Gold@75%), Golden Sand→White→Light Blue (#FEF1C8→#ffffff→#E6F0F9), Golden Sand→75% Gold→Sunshine (#FEF1C8→Gold@75%→#faa31b), Sunshine→light gold masthead gradient (#faa31b→#ffe05c).

findings.description: Heading explicitly states 'approved set — never invent gradients', a strong prohibitive governance constraint (hard/negative polarity, forbidding creation of new gradients outside the approved list). No rule captures this closed-list/no-invention governance facet.

payload.rule_text: Only the approved gradients listed may be used; never invent new gradients.

findings.description: 'Approved values only' is a distinct hard-constraint statement about usage (backgrounds, wave graphic, logo mark combos) that needs its own governance facet (hard, positive/prescriptive scope-limiting) rather than being folded away or omitted.

payload.rule_text: Gradients are used as backgrounds, in combination with the wave graphic, and with the LISRAYA logo mark; approved values only.

findings.description: The masthead gradient row contains a specific compositional constraint (mark sits at the lightest end for contrast) that is a distinct relational/placement rule tied to logo usage, separate from the general gradient definition — this relation to logo placement rules is missing.

payload.rule_text: In the masthead gradient (#faa31b → light gold), the logo mark must sit at the lightest end of the gradient for contrast.

findings.description: Directionality rule (dark/saturated → light, left → right, horizontal only in masthead/banner) is a separate orientation constraint that should be its own rule rather than merged into gradient color definitions.

payload.rule_text: In masthead/banner applications, gradients run horizontally from dark/saturated to light, left to right.
